name: A Coruna — circular de Torre de Hercules
name_original: A Coruña — circular de Torre de Hércules
region_intro: Jogging around A Coruña offers diverse routes along its striking Atlantic coastline, through numerous urban green spaces, and past historic landmarks. The city features one of Europe's longest seaside promenades, expansive urban beaches, and parks like Bens Park. Runners can explore varied terrain from paved coastal paths to softer trails within parks, all set against the backdrop of the Atlantic Ocean. This blend of urban and natural environments provides a range of options for different running preferences and fitness levels. Best jogging routes around A Coruña The most popular jogging route is A Coruna — circular de Torre de Hercules, a 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail that takes 29 minutes to complete. This easy route circles the historic Tower of Hercules, offering panoramic coastal views. Another top favourite among local runners is Bens Park and Coastal Path Loop, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This route combines ocean views with diverse terrain through Bens Park and along the coast. Local runners also love the Orzán Beach – A Coruña Seafront Promenade loop from Estadio ABANCA-RIAZOR, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through urban beaches and along the extensive promenade, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Jogging around A Coruña is defined by coastal paths, urban beaches, and green spaces, offering options for different ability levels. The routes in A Coruña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 4000 runners have used komoot to explore A Coruña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in A Coruña?
A: There are nearly 90 running routes around A Coruña, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 80 reviews.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on A Coruña's jogging paths?
A: A Coruña offers diverse terrain for runners. You'll find extensive paved coastal paths like the &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Marítimo&lt;/strong&gt;, which stretches for approximately 15 kilometers and often includes dedicated running lanes. There are also expansive urban beaches such as Riazor and Orzán for soft sand running, and numerous green spaces like Bens Park, San Pedro, San Diego, and Santa Margarita Parks, which provide softer trails amidst unique tree species and sculptures.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, A Coruña has several easy running routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984114782/a-coruna-circular-de-torre-de-hercules" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Coruna — circular de Torre de Hercules&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.3 km (2.7 miles) trail that circles the historic Tower of Hercules, offering panoramic coastal views. Overall, there are 12 easy routes available in the region.

Q: Where can I find long-distance running paths in A Coruña?
A: For longer runs, the &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Marítimo&lt;/strong&gt; is an excellent choice. It's one of Europe's longest seaside promenades, and a full loop around the peninsula, from Oza to Bens beach, can provide a roughly 20-kilometer route, akin to a half-marathon course. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984108835/bens-park-and-coastal-path-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bens Park and Coastal Path Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10 km (6.2 miles) and combines ocean views with diverse terrain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in A Coruña?
A: Yes, many of A Coruña's running routes are circular. Besides the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984114782/a-coruna-circular-de-torre-de-hercules" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Coruna — circular de Torre de Hercules&lt;/a&gt;, you can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40342345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orzán Beach – A Coruña Seafront Promenade loop from Estadio ABANCA-RIAZOR&lt;/a&gt;, a 10 km (6.2 miles) loop along urban beaches and the promenade. Bens Park also features a 6.2-mile circular trail with ocean views.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see while running in A Coruña?
A: A Coruña's running routes often pass by significant landmarks and natural features. You can run around the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1860479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Hercules&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, and see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Compass Rose&lt;/a&gt; nearby. The coastal routes offer views of urban beaches like Riazor and Orzán, and you might spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1247339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Surfers' Fountain&lt;/a&gt;. Green spaces like San Carlos Garden also provide pleasant scenery.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more challenging run, A Coruña offers routes with significant elevation gain and varied terrain. While the guide highlights 17 difficult routes, the region research mentions the "Entre Dúas Rías Trail" as an example of a route with coastal views and forest paths that includes notable climbs.

Q: Can I find running routes that are accessible by public transport?
A: A Coruña is a well-connected city, and many of its urban and coastal running routes are easily accessible by public transport. The extensive &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Marítimo&lt;/strong&gt; and routes around the city center or major parks like Bens Park are generally well-served by the local bus network, making it convenient to reach starting points without a car.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in A Coruña?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning coastal views, the variety of terrain from paved promenades to soft park trails, and the unique experience of running past historic landmarks like the Tower of Hercules. The dedicated running lanes on the Paseo Marítimo are also frequently highlighted for providing a safe and enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly scenic or offer great viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The routes along the &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Marítimo&lt;/strong&gt; and around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1860479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Hercules&lt;/a&gt; are renowned for their stunning panoramic views of the Atlantic Ocean. Bens Park also features trails with beautiful ocean vistas. These coastal paths provide continuous picturesque scenery, making for a very enjoyable and visually rewarding run.

Q: Are there any parks in A Coruña suitable for running?
A: Yes, A Coruña is rich in green spaces ideal for running. &lt;strong&gt;Bens Park&lt;/strong&gt; offers a 6.2-mile circular trail with ocean views and diverse terrain. Other notable parks include San Pedro, San Diego, and Santa Margarita Parks, which provide kilometers of trails amidst unique tree species and sculptures, offering a softer and healthier surface for runners.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in A Coruña?
A: A Coruña's mild climate generally makes it suitable for running year-round. However, the spring and autumn months often offer the most comfortable temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be warm, especially midday, while winters are generally mild but can bring rain. Checking the local weather forecast before heading out is always recommended.
tips:
Group of twelve sculptures inaugurated in 2003 in the 'Campo de la Rata'. Created by the Galician sculptor Manolo Paz, they share a location with the historical shootings carried out by the Franco authorities at the beginning of the Spanish Civil War.

The set of menhir stones represent the family, the largest for the parents and the smallest for the children. On the other hand, they are strategically placed in the shape of a circle, specifically, two circles, one inside the other.

Gratuitous

Conch shell, 1994 (Moncho Amigo, A Coruña, 1952)

It is the fantastic recreation of the shell of a huge mollusk that takes the form of a cornucopia, in which the sounds of the sea are kept. It is located in Punta Herminia, a place especially exposed to inclement weather and the sea, which is why it has been recently restored. Like the Menhirs of Peace, the Conch has blended with the surrounding landscape of the Tower.

ARTABRO (Arturo Andrade)
Woman, sailor and warrior, they express the desires and efforts of a combative people.
